behavior_summary: NPC 'Corinth' provides a short U8P_CORINTH::look/label that depends on whether the player has been met (corinthMet).
Corinth runs a time-of-day driven U8P_CORINTH::schedule() that chooses and persists a corinthSchedule value and drives location/behavior choices.

When the player enters the NPC's fast-area trigger (U8P_CORINTH::enterFastArea) the script resets engine refs and dispatches a U8P_CORINTH::schedule-specific routine: corinthSchedule == 0x01 -> U8P_CORINTH::func0364, corinthSchedule == 0x02 -> U8P_CORINTH::func09B5.

The U8P_CORINTH::schedule-specific utilities (U8P_CORINTH::func0364 and U8P_CORINTH::func09B5) inspect an item/target, attempt Npc::pathfind to it, and on success call monologue routines (U8P_CORINTH::func04DC or U8P_CORINTH::func0B2D) to emit short barks; failures U8P_CORINTH::use short-lived spawned helpers as fallbacks.

U8P_CORINTH::AvatarStoleSomething(uword) reacts to nearby thefts: it inspects Item::getType, checks guardsCalled and stolenOnce, warns the player with barks, may set stolenOnce for a specific valuable type, spawn helper processes, and call CALGUARD::guardRespondToCrime to summon guards.

The conversation handler (process U8P_CORINTH::use()) builds and updates a dynamic menu, personalizes lines with the player's name, branches on corinthMet/corinthPissed/corinthRealPissed and the player's menu choices, sets flags (e.g., corinthMet, corinthRealPissed) and ends or continues the conversation accordingly.
notable_control_flow: U8P_CORINTH::schedule() guards early with Npc::isDead and branches its parameter set on FREE::getTimeOfDayPeriod (morning/afternoon vs evening/night), then calls METHOD::func143A and uses process_result() to decide whether to persist corinthSchedule and call METHOD::func133F.

U8P_CORINTH::enterFastArea() resets several engine references (FREE::resetRef ids 0x0200/0x0204/0x0205) and conditionally calls U8P_CORINTH::func0364 or U8P_CORINTH::func09B5 based on corinthSchedule.

U8P_CORINTH::func0364 and U8P_CORINTH::func09B5 follow the same pattern: read item frame, loop guarded by a bounds check (local2 <= 0x1B), request Npc::pathfind, suspend, check process_result(), then either call the monologue (U8P_CORINTH::func04DC/U8P_CORINTH::func0B2D) when pathfind succeeds and nobody is talking, or spawn a short helper (FREE::func1C19) as a fallback on failure or if somebody is talking.

U8P_CORINTH::func04DC and U8P_CORINTH::func0B2D compute a 3D distance (FREE::maxDelta3D) and choose between close/far bark sets; selection within sets uses urandom to pick a line and interleaves METHOD::func091C/METHOD::func15E7 and suspend points for speech synchronization.

U8P_CORINTH::AvatarStoleSomething checks Item::getType for explicit type values (notably 0x021D) and uses guardsCalled to avoid repeated guard dispatch; special-case first-time valuable theft sets stolenOnce and triggers a distinct reaction, otherwise it barks and calls CALGUARD::guardRespondToCrime.
open_questions: What exact menu entries and text-to-flag mappings in U8P_CORINTH::use() correspond to corinthPissed vs corinthRealPissed branches (menu text and choice strings are present but full mapping is low-confidence).
What does U8P_CORINTH::func02BA (called from U8P_CORINTH::AvatarStoleSomething) perform beyond being a reaction helper (animation, additional speech, or quest logic)?
Precise numeric meanings of corinthSchedule values (beyond 0x01 and 0x02 selecting different routines) and how they map to world positions/routes.
Source and authoritative check for somebodyTalking boolean (where it is set/read outside this script) and how it interacts with speech reservation behavior.
Exact spawn arguments and lifecycle for FREE::func1C19 helper processes used as fallbacks (timing and purpose inferred but not explicit).

main_function.name: U8P_CORINTH::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short description shown when the player examines the NPC; selects between a proper name 'Corinth' or a generic 'Woman' label based on whether the player has met the NPC (global flag corinthMet).
entry_conditions: Invoked by the engine when the player uses the 'U8P_CORINTH::look' (examine) action on this NPC.
No parameters; reads the global flag corinthMet to choose which short label to emit.

function_map.name: U8P_CORINTH::AvatarStoleSomething
function_map.role: theft/crime response (handles stolen item event, barks, guard summon)

function_map.name: U8P_CORINTH::schedule
function_map.role: daily U8P_CORINTH::schedule evaluator (sets corinthSchedule and triggers movement/state)

function_map.name: U8P_CORINTH::enterFastArea
function_map.role: fast-area entry trigger: reset refs and dispatch U8P_CORINTH::schedule-specific routine

function_map.name: U8P_CORINTH::leaveFastArea
function_map.role: fast-area exit trigger: cleanup/reset engine reference

function_map.name: U8P_CORINTH::func0364
function_map.role: U8P_CORINTH::schedule-specific utility (pathfind to item/target; on success call U8P_CORINTH::func04DC)

function_map.name: U8P_CORINTH::func04DC
function_map.role: monologue/bark emitter for U8P_CORINTH::schedule A (distance-based random barks)

function_map.name: U8P_CORINTH::func09B5
function_map.role: U8P_CORINTH::schedule-specific utility for alternate U8P_CORINTH::schedule (mirrors U8P_CORINTH::func0364, then calls U8P_CORINTH::func0B2D)

function_map.name: U8P_CORINTH::func0B2D
function_map.role: monologue/bark emitter for U8P_CORINTH::schedule B (distance-based random barks variant)

function_map.name: U8P_CORINTH::func02BA
function_map.role: NPC-specific reaction helper called after special-case theft branch (inferred; not fully disassembled here)

function_map.name: U8P_CORINTH::use
function_map.role: full conversation/dialog handler (menu construction, personalization, flag setting)

internal_calls.purpose: Dispatch alternate U8P_CORINTH::schedule-specific behavior when corinthSchedule == 0x02
On successful pathfind and speech availability, call monologue routine to emit barks
On successful pathfind and speech availability, call alternate monologue routine to emit barks
Invoke NPC-specific reaction for the special first-time valuable theft branch
None (no direct call); listed for completeness — U8P_CORINTH::schedule routines are mutually exclusive based on corinthSchedule

external_calls.purpose: Query current time-of-day period to select U8P_CORINTH::schedule parameter set
Lookup/query used during U8P_CORINTH::schedule evaluation (likely to choose route/target); result checked via process_result()
Apply chosen U8P_CORINTH::schedule/state to the NPC (move/animation/state commit)
Clear/rebind engine references (ids 0x0200/0x0204/0x0205) on fast-area entry
Clear engine reference (id 0x0205) on fast-area exit
Request NPC to path to a target item/location; result determines speech vs fallback
Same as above for alternate U8P_CORINTH::schedule routine
Compute 3D distance between positions to choose close/far bark sets
Prepare/reserve speech channel before emitting certain barks
Synchronization/delay helper used around speech/suspend points
Notify/dispatch town guards in response to thefts
Spawn short-lived helper process as a fallback or timeout in theft handling
Spawn short-lived helper process in pathfind failure or somebody-talking fallback branches

reads.name: corinthMet
reads.likely_effect: Controls U8P_CORINTH::look() label selection and influences initial dialog/menu text (whether the NPC recognizes the player).

reads.name: guardsCalled
reads.likely_effect: Used to prevent repeatedly calling guards for the same theft incident.

reads.name: corinthSchedule
reads.likely_effect: Read by U8P_CORINTH::enterFastArea to select which U8P_CORINTH::schedule-specific routine to run (mapping observed for values 0x01 and 0x02).

reads.name: corinthPissed
reads.likely_effect: Read by U8P_CORINTH::use() to alter initial greeting/menu when the NPC is annoyed.

reads.name: corinthRealPissed
reads.likely_effect: Read by U8P_CORINTH::use() to select an alternate branch (skip normal greeting); can be set by dialogue choices.

writes.name: stolenOnce
writes.likely_effect: Set when a specific valuable (item type 0x021D) is stolen to change future theft responses and avoid repeating the special warning.

writes.name: corinthSchedule
writes.likely_effect: Persist the chosen daily U8P_CORINTH::schedule/state so other behaviors (U8P_CORINTH::enterFastArea) can run the appropriate routine.

writes.name: corinthMet
writes.likely_effect: Set by conversation (U8P_CORINTH::use()) when the player identifies themselves to mark the NPC as having met the player.

writes.name: corinthRealPissed
writes.likely_effect: Set when the player selects a very rude dialogue option; alters future dialog/behavior.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: NPC 'Corinth' provides a short U8P_CORINTH::look/label that depends on whether the player has been met (corinthMet).
